channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democrats ask for an investigation into DOGE's access to Treasury's payment systems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic lawmakers are seeking a Treasury Department investigation of the access that Elon Musk's team was given to the government's payment system, citing “threats to the economy and national security, and the potential violation of laws protecting Americans’ privacy and tax dat
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Members of Congress denied access to Department of Education
item.description: Multiple members of Congress were denied entry to the Department of Education on Friday, according to videos and social media posts from lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order imposing sanctions on International Criminal Court over investigations of Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order imposing sanctions on the International Criminal Court over investigations of Israel, a close U.S. ally. Neither the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department says it won't release the names of FBI agents who worked on Jan. 6 riot cases
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has agreed to refrain from publicly identifying any FBI agents whose conduct is under review as President Donald Trump’s administration examines the investigations into the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: A federal judge is to consider whether to block DOGE access to the US Labor Department
item.description: A federal judge will consider whether to block Trump adviser Elon Musk’s team from accessing systems at the Labor Department, which has investigated the billionaire’s companies.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats try to enter Department of Education amid outrage over possible DOGE cuts
item.description: Democrat lawmakers attempted to enter the Department of Education building in Washington, but were blocked by a security guard who did not detail why he would not let them enter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP-led House panel to hold hearing on USAID
item.description: The GOP-led House Foreign Affairs Committee will hold a hearing on the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) on Feb. 13, as the Trump administration moved to effectively shut down the agency and institute a mass foreign aid freeze. Committee Chair Brian Mast (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba hold joint news conference
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba came to the White House on Friday near midday and was expected to spend a little more than two hours with Trump for a working lunch and a joint news conference before making the return trip to Tokyo.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NASA instructs employees to remove pronouns from all work communications
item.description: An email seen by NPR says the move is to comply with a presidential order to “restore biological truth” to the government.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel, MPR News

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis backs Trump’s plans for FEMA, DOGE efforts
item.description: The governor announced his support for President Donald Trump looking to remove FEMA as a middleman after a disaster.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker pushes bold legislation to protect homeownership from corporations
item.description: In what's being called a "bold new measure," one Tampa Bay lawmaker wants to put families over corporate elites by making sure local Floridians can stay in their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Joe Rogan cheers Musk’s ‘business approach’ to government cuts
item.description: “What’s fascinating right now is we’re getting a chance to see what happens when you take a business approach to the government,” Rogan said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Ben Stiller denies being paid by USAID to go to Ukraine
item.description: The reportedly debunked social media post was shared by billionaire Elon Musk, who owns X, and Donald Trump Jr..
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr. pass committee hurdles in win for Trump
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday cleared crucial hurdles toward winning Senate confirmation, handing President Trump a key win as he looks for an early display of loyalty from GOP lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Nassau police to help ICE detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes
item.description: NASSAU COUNTY, N.Y. (PIX11) – The Nassau County Police Department will be directly participating in ICE enforcement to detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes, County Executive Bruce Blakeman announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spent some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Purges at FBI, DOJ trigger 'battle' for career staff
item.description: The Trump administration has removed dozens of Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI officials and is eyeing firing perhaps thousands more in an unprecedented purge that may just be getting started.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Donald Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, Fox 5

item.title: Schools poised to be major front line in Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: Oklahoma is moving to require proof of citizenship for the guardians of public school students, while California schools are distributing "know your rights" cards to immigrant families as K-12 campuses become a crucial front line in President Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Legislature observes 'family day,' targets high price of eggs as session starts
item.description: State lawmakers convened in Carson City on Monday, beginning the 83rd Legislative Session with the legislative chambers filled with family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Trump, Mexican president agree to delay tariffs for one month
item.description: President Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said they have agreed to delay tariffs by at least one month, following a call between the two leaders Monday. The 25 percent tariffs on Mexico were to be implemented Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Donald Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis, Florida GOP tensions spill out into open
item.description: Tensions are spilling out into the open between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) and members of his own party in the Sunshine State, pointing to a new discordant era of GOP-led politics in a state that has trended red over the last few cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis threatens Veto amid heated showdown
item.description: State lawmakers voted to pass the TRUMP act, which aims to support the president on immigration enforcement. Now, all eyes are on Governor DeSantis who promises a veto.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Political parties explain Hillsborough County's conservative flip
item.description: As of Thursday, Hillsborough County records showed the number of active registered Republicans was bigger than the number of registered Democrats. The difference as of Friday was 307 voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
